comment_text.sn35.64:1.3: Despite the Buddha’s reservations in the previous discourse, it is normal for mendicants to withdraw into retreat for dedicated practice. Here, it seems, Migajāla determines to practice living alone in both ways, as he was taught in the previous Sutta.

root_text.sn35.64:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā migajālo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe…
root_text.sn35.64:1.2: ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā migajālo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.64:1.3: “sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā saṅkhittena dhammaṁ desetu, yamahaṁ bhagavato dhammaṁ sutvā eko vūpakaṭṭho appamatto ātāpī pahitatto vihareyyan”ti.
root_text.sn35.64:2.1: “Santi kho, migajāla, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.64:2.2: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn35.64:2.3: Tassa taṁ abhinandato abhivadato ajjhosāya tiṭṭhato uppajjati nandī.
root_text.sn35.64:2.5: santi ca kho, migajāla, jivhāviññeyyā rasā …pe…
root_text.sn35.64:2.6: santi ca kho, migajāla, manoviññeyyā dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.64:2.7: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn35.64:2.8: Tassa taṁ abhinandato abhivadato ajjhosāya tiṭṭhato uppajjati nandī.
root_text.sn35.64:3.1: Santi ca kho, migajāla, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.64:3.2: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati. Tassa taṁ anabhinandato anabhivadato anajjhosāya tiṭṭhato nandī nirujjhati.
root_text.sn35.64:3.4: santi ca kho, migajāla, jivhāviññeyyā rasā iṭṭhā kantā …pe…
root_text.sn35.64:3.5: santi ca kho, migajāla, manoviññeyyā dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.64:3.6: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati. Tassa taṁ anabhinandato anabhivadato anajjhosāya tiṭṭhato nandī nirujjhati.
root_text.sn35.64:4.1: Atha kho āyasmā migajālo bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.
root_text.sn35.64:4.2: Atha kho āyasmā migajālo eko vūpakaṭṭho appamatto ātāpī pahitatto viharato nacirasseva—yassatthāya kulaputtā sammadeva agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajanti, tadanuttaraṁ—brahmacariyapariyosānaṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihāsi.
root_text.sn35.64:4.3: “Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā”ti abbhaññāsi.
root_text.sn35.64:4.4: Aññataro ca panāyasmā migajālo arahataṁ ahosīti.

translation_text.sn35.64:1.1: Then Venerable Migajāla went up to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn35.64:1.3: “Sir, may the Buddha please teach me Dhamma in brief. When I’ve heard it, I’ll live alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute.”
translation_text.sn35.64:2.1: “Migajāla, there are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.64:2.2: If a mendicant approves, welcomes, and keep clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.64:2.4: Relishing is the origin of suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn35.64:2.5: There are sounds … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.64:2.6: ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.64:2.7: If a mendicant approves, welcomes, and keeps clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.64:2.9: Relishing is the origin of suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn35.64:3.1: There are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.64:3.2: If a mendicant doesn’t approve, welcome, and keep clinging to them, relishing ceases.
translation_text.sn35.64:3.3: When relishing ceases, suffering ceases, I say.
translation_text.sn35.64:3.4: There are sounds … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.64:3.5: ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.64:3.6: If a mendicant doesn’t approve, welcome, and keep clinging to them, relishing ceases.
translation_text.sn35.64:3.7: When relishing ceases, suffering ceases, I say.”
translation_text.sn35.64:4.1: And then Venerable Migajāla approved and agreed with what the Buddha said. He got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right, before leaving.
translation_text.sn35.64:4.2: Then Migajāla, living alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute, soon realized the supreme end of the spiritual path in this very life. He lived having achieved with his own insight the goal for which gentlemen rightly go forth from the lay life to homelessness.
translation_text.sn35.64:4.3: He understood: “Rebirth is ended; the spiritual journey has been completed; what had to be done has been done; there is nothing further for this place.”
translation_text.sn35.64:4.4: And Migajāla became one of the perfected.
